Ottilia Review - Empires & Puzzles Gnome Decorator Strategy Guide

She's tiny, she's festive, and she's surprisingly sticky. Our deep dive into Ottilia explores whether this gnome's Very Fast buff copying is a game-changer or if she's just a decorative ornament on your bench. We analyze her unique sustain mechanics and compare her 2024 stats to the 2026 titans.
Ottilia: The Mistletoe Sniper Who Copies Your Homework
Ottilia, The Gnome Decorator: Very Fast Sniping and Buff Copying

Ottilia is a 5-star Legendary Fire hero from the Winter Family (Santa's Challenge), originally released in December 2024. As a "Gnome Decorator," she brings a mix of festive cheer and surprisingly aggressive utility. She fits the archetype of a

with a unique twist: instead of just dealing damage, she acts as a selfish support, copying enemy buffs to sustain herself. In an era dominated by heavy hitters and complex passives, Ottilia offers a simpler, speed-focused approach. She doesn't have a costume yet, so this review focuses on her base form. If you think decorating is just about hanging lights, wait until she decorates herself with your enemy's best buffs.

Ottilia embraces the "cute but dangerous" aesthetic. She is depicted as a small gnome holding a festive sprig of mistletoe, dressed in a traditional red winter dress. The art style is vibrant and leans into the whimsical side of the Winter event, contrasting sharply with the more menacing designs of recent Astral or Covenant heroes. While she might look like she belongs on a greeting card, that mischievous grin suggests she knows exactly what she's doing—probably plotting to steal (or copy) your lunch money.
Ottilia's stats reflect her late 2024 release window. While they were considered "premium" upon her debut, the relentless march of power creep through 2025 has shifted her standing.

, Ottilia has fallen behind the curve of the latest 2026 heroes, who regularly push 1250+ Power and 1300+ stats. However, she is far from obsolete. Her stats are still significantly higher than classic heroes and older event heroes, keeping her sturdy enough to survive a few hits in modern wars. Her
speed mitigates the stat gap somewhat, as she can often fire before the heavy hitters charge up.
"Mistletoe Medley" is a skill that prioritizes speed and self-sustain over raw team utility.
speed is respectable. It's a classic sniper hit—enough to severely dent a squishy support or finish off a weakened tank, though it won't one-shot a healthy modern hero.

buffs (not stealing) allows her to mirror the enemy's strength. If the enemy tank has a massive Defense Up or Counterattack, Ottilia gets it too. However, since she
, the enemy retains their advantage, which limits her disruption potential.
Recovering 10% health per copied buff (up to 50%) is a nice bonus, turning her into a self-sustaining annoyance. It rewards you for targeting heavily buffed enemies.

The Elf Minion is largely decorative in the modern meta. At 10% stats, it's easily swept away by modern AOE damage or minion counters, serving mostly as a tiny meat shield or a buffer against ailments.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Winter family is one of the older families, and it shows in the simplicity of its bonuses.

The bonus improves Minion stats. Since Ottilia summons minions for herself, this adds a tiny bit of survivability. However, given how weak Elf minions are by 2026 standards, this bonus is negligible unless you are running a full "minion spam" team (e.g., with Pengi or Mother North).
This is Ottilia's biggest weakness. Modern heroes come with innate resistance, mana generation stacks, or damage reduction. Ottilia has to rely entirely on her special skill and raw stats.
gives her a +20% Attack boost for the first 6 turns. This fits her "blitz" playstyle perfectly. It helps her secure kills early in the match, which is critical for a Very Fast sniper.

Upon release in late 2024, Ottilia was seen as a solid, if not meta-breaking, option. She was praised for her high stats (at the time) and speed but criticized for the "copying" mechanic being inferior to "stealing" (like the Goblin heroes or older heroes like Hatter).
By 2026, her stats are "average" for a legendary. Her lack of passives hurts her durability and utility compared to the feature-rich heroes of the Astral or Construct families.
but niche. She is no longer a top-tier defense candidate. Her home is now in
(paradoxically, as she is already fast, but the stats help) or as a specialized counter in

. She is a great depth hero for War teams, specifically for taking out key targets before they can wreck your team, but she won't carry a battle single-handedly against a full team of modern monsters.
Ottilia currently does not have a costume.

If you pulled Ottilia, celebrate! A Very Fast sniper is one of the most useful tools for a developing roster. She will serve you well in almost all map stages and raids. Focus on getting her to 3/70 immediately, and she's a worthy candidate for Rings if you lack a modern 2026 heavy hitter.

Ottilia is a luxury toy. She's fun and effective in specific war matchups (like cleaning up teams), but she shouldn't displace your top-tier projects. Do not waste Alpha Aethers on her unless you are a huge fan of the art or desperate for a Very Fast red option. There are 4-star heroes with better utility now.
When released, the community was vocal about her "Copy" skill. Many users, like
on the forums, noted, "Stealing would be thematic, Grinch-like behavior." The consensus was that copying is strictly worse than stealing (as seen on heroes like Deadboot).
Players were shocked she launched with zero passives in late 2024. "Oh, she has zero passives? Didn't know they even made them that way anymore. Weak," remarked one forum user.
While initially underwhelming to those chasing the meta, many found her damage surprisingly adequate for her speed. She settled into a role of "Good, not Great."

Ottilia is like that one Christmas ornament you didn't ask for but looks surprisingly nice on the tree. She won't revolutionize your gameplay or dominate the leaderboards, but she does her job—hitting fast and staying alive—reliably. In a game that often overcomplicates things with paragraphs of text, sometimes you just need a gnome to throw a snowball at someone's face
A solid B-tier hero who earns her keep through speed, but shows her age through her lack of passives and older stats. Keep her for depth, but don't build your empire around her.
Is Ottilia still worth leveling in 2026? A detailed review of the Very Fast Winter hero, analyzing her sniping, buff copying mechanics, and viability in the modern meta.
